title: SLURM Resource Mapping
description: Schema for config/compute-profiles/slurm-mapping.yaml. Maps abstract ResourceRequirements from the emitted compute-resource-policy.json to SLURM partition/QoS/gres/time flags. Site-specific: each cluster ships its own YAML. Validated at load; drift fails loud at executor startup, not at sbatch time.

version.description: Schema version. Bump when the shape changes in a way that breaks old YAML.

resource_classes.description: Named resource classes. Keys are arbitrary identifiers (e.g. cpu_small, gpu_large); the sizing loader picks the smallest class whose SLURM-flagged resources satisfy the stage's ResourceRequirements.

fallback.description: Used when no resource_class satisfies the ResourceRequirements. Required so the loader never has to error at runtime — operators pick the safest default for their site.

partitions.description: Plan §S15.12 — per-partition container-runtime allow-list. Keys are partition names referenced by resource_classes[*].partition above; values declare which container runtimes the partition supports. `slurm/sbatch.rs::validate_submission` rejects with `BlockerKind::SlurmRuntimeUnavailable` when an atom's `preferred_container` requires a runtime not on the partition's list. Optional: when omitted, the validator falls back to the agent script's auto-detect chain (S15.4).

items.description: Runtime keyword. `none` means 'host execution allowed' (no container required); `apptainer-1.4`, `singularity-3.x`, `podman`, `docker` follow the agent-claude-slurm.sh probe priority.

mem.description: --mem= value in SLURM's syntax (e.g. '16G', '64000M'). Integer + optional K/M/G/T suffix.

time.description: --time= wall-clock limit in SLURM syntax (DD-HH:MM:SS or HH:MM:SS).
